role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Focused on script and bank name etymology.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Identified tree species and urban landscaping.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Analyzed traffic flow and vehicle types.

visual_consistency_notes.linguistic: Correctly identified Arabic and Latin text.
visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Accurately described white buildings and vegetation.
visual_consistency_notes.meta: Correctly identified the yellow taxi and fence style.

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The agents unanimously identified Tunisia based on strong linguistic evidence (Arabic/French bank signage) and corroborating visual cues (white architecture, yellow taxis). The judge correctly followed Path A (consensus) and made the right decision.
verdict.region_narrowing_notes: Perfect consensus among all five agents on the region (North Africa/Maghreb) and country (Tunisia). The reasoning was sound and evidence-based.
verdict.hypothesis_pool_notes: The pool included Tunisia, Algeria, and Morocco. While the truth (Tunisia) was present, the pool was limited to the Maghreb region. Given the strong linguistic evidence, a more diverse pool (e.g., including Lebanon or Egypt) might have been more rigorous, but the Maghreb pool was visually plausible.
